sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Terminology 16:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Terminology 16:1
items.html: The illumination that operates in a Partzuf when it has no vessels of reception for that light is called “illumination from afar.” It means that when there is a great distance and difference between the light and the vessels of the Partzuf related to that light, the vessels cannot receive and clothe that light. Instead, they receive from it an illumination from afar.
